title: Cache Performance, Scope, Integrity, and Poisoning Defense · Automation Case: Ci Cold Warm Performance Evidence
summary: A privileged release restores an executable cache written from a fork, accepts broad restore keys, hides cold-install failures, and attributes network time to compilation. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ci cold warm performance evidence increment in cache threat and measurement policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Measure cold and warm dependency, build, test, upload, queue, and critical-path time while preserving a regular cold-path regression run.
Use setup-node dependency caching without caching node_modules and disable automatic caching in privileged workflows when risk outweighs speed.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.

version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0

  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example privileged release restores executable cache content written by a fork through a broad fallback key. Compare cold and warm time, key scope, save authority, poisoning risk, and immutable artifact separation. Competency probe: Measure cold and warm dependency, build, test, upload, queue, and critical-path time while preserving a regular cold-path regression run. A defensible solution applies Ci Cold Warm Performance Evidence, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to cache threat and measurement policy.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-worked-gha-cache-performance-integrity-5
  - competency: ci-worked-gha-cache-performance-integrity-5
    artifact: cache-policy.yaml
    decision: worked gha cache performance integrity 5
    environment: single-job ephemeral self-hosted runner in a segmented subnet
    fault-injection: expire the only retained test artifact during incident reconstruction
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: worked5
    change: |
      cache-content: npm-downloads-only
      key: os-arch-node24-lockhash-config
      restore-prefix: exact-trusted-branch
      writer: trusted-push-only
      untrusted-reader: bounded-read
      node-modules: excluded
      privileged-release-cache: disabled
      cold-run: scheduled
      measurement: queue-install-build-test-upload
    verify: compare cold warm and poisoned-key fixtures while checking writer event scope restored key and critical path
    failure: Reject cache as provenance, executable fork-writable caches, broad restore keys, node_modules reuse, or warm-only evidence.
    repair: Cache disposable inputs, bind keys and writers, isolate trust domains, retain cold runs, and measure the actual critical path.
content.caption: Runnable CI/CD workflow, artifact, release, and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A warm-cache improvement proves the complete pipeline is faster. This fails the Ci Cold Warm Performance Evidence contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
